definition_population.content: Immunodeficiency 18 is caused by biallelic CD3E mutations disrupting the CD3-epsilon subunit of the TCR complex, blocking T-cell development and producing a SCID-like phenotype in infancy.

sources.quote: Homozygous mutations in CD3D and CD3E genes lead to a complete block in

epidemiology.content: No disease-specific prevalence/incidence figure found; reported via individual case reports and small national SCID cohort series.

sources.quote: Biallelic Form of a Known CD3E Mutation in a Patient with Severe Combined Immunodeficiency.
Mutational landscape of severe combined immunodeficiency patients from Turkey.

biology.content: CD3-epsilon is required for TCR/CD3 complex assembly and surface expression; homozygous CD3D/CD3E mutations block T-cell development; related CD3-delta deficiency produces profound T-cell depletion.

treatment.content: No FDA-approved disease-specific pharmacologic therapy identified in this pass; standard-of-care for CD3-subunit SCID-type disease is hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, per general SCID management literature (no CD3E-specific drug application found).

regulatory_trials.content: ClinicalTrials.gov: CD3-deficiency-specific search returned 1 total trial; broader SCID condition returned 115 total trials.

sources.quote: total=1 (condition=CD3 deficiency severe combined immunodeficiency); total=115 (condition=severe combined immunodeficiency)

gaps: No FDA drug application specific to CD3E deficiency found
